parser_lock_point.description: Parser correctness/perf checkpoint. grammargen-next must not regress against this baseline.

reduced_frontier.description: 6-language breaker set: small enough to run constantly, ugly enough to catch regressions. cpp stays opt-in until its focused perf lane is trustworthy.

gate0_compiler_health.description: The compiler still works: import, generate, emit, no panics/OOM.

gate1_parser_correctness.description: Hard stop. No new fails, no pass→drift, no timeout/killed, no highlight init breakage.

gate2_promotion.description: Grammargen earns promotion into parser-locked lane only after Gate 1.

promotion_rule: Grammargen may merge forward within grammargen-next if it passes Gate 0 and does not regress the locked reduced frontier. It may merge into the parser-locked lane only if it is non-regressing on the reduced frontier, non-regressing on parser canaries (Gate 1), and neutral-or-better on the by-language real-corpus board (Gate 2).
